text:
AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-07

今日结论：
The dominant pattern today is a clear shift toward AI Agent Frameworks, which now account for 6 of the top 10 projects and the highest average weekly delta (1,962 stars). CopilotKit saw a sudden breakout with a 6.7x acceleration, adding 799 stars in 24 hours, likely driven by its multi-platform SDK for agent-native apps. CowAgent also entered the top 10 with a 2.1x acceleration, signaling growing interest in open-source agent harnesses. Meanwhile, Coding Agents like DeepSeek-Reasonix remain hot but are no longer accelerating, suggesting attention is rotating toward broader frameworks. The MCP / Tool Calling category, which led yesterday, has cooled, with no projects in the top 10 today. This rotation may indicate developers are moving from tool-level abstractions to full agent orchestration layers.

与昨日对比：
  + 新进 Top 10: zhayujie/CowAgent, thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist, PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR, affaan-m/ECC, HKUDS/nanobot
  - 退出 Top 10: Panniantong/Agent-Reach, mvanhorn/last30days-skill, MemPalace/mempalace, safishamsi/graphify, lfnovo/open-notebook
  方向变化: MCP / Tool Calling -> AI Agent Framework

今日精选项目
1. zhayujie/CowAgent
   GitHub: https://github.com/zhayujie/CowAgent
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 45,093 (24h +33, 7d +143)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 16)
   Score: 68 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: CowAgent is an open-source AI agent harness that plans tasks, runs tools and skills, and grows with memory and knowledge, supporting multiple LLMs and messaging channels.
   Why worth watching: With 45,093 stars, a 24-hour delta of +33, and an acceleration ratio of 2.1 (high), CowAgent is experiencing a sudden breakout in attention. The project has a recent release (v2.1.0 on 2026-06-01) and active commits within the last 7 days, indicating sustained development momentum.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward integrated agent harnesses that handle planning, memory, and tool use out of the box, lowering the barrier for developers to deploy autonomous AI assistants in production across multiple platforms.
   Problem solved: It provides a lightweight, extensible framework for building autonomous AI assistants that can plan and execute complex tasks, integrate with various tools and services, and maintain long-term memory and knowledge, all deployable with a one-line installer.
   What changed: The recent v2.1.0 release introduced internationalization, new channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeChat Customer Service), CLI upgrades, streamlined one-line install, and MCP Streamable HTTP support, likely driving the sudden spike in stars.
   Target users: Developers and teams building AI-powered assistants for personal or enterprise use, especially those needing multi-channel deployment (WeChat, Telegram, Slack, etc.) and support for multiple LLM providers.
   Risk notes: The project is relatively new (v2.1.0) and may have undiscovered bugs or stability issues. Dependency on external LLM APIs and MCP servers could introduce latency or cost. The rapid growth may attract attention but also increase maintenance burden.
   LLM confidence: high

2. thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist
   GitHub: https://github.com/thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 72,841 (24h +25, 7d +93)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 2.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 12)
   Score: 60 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A comprehensive, MCP-powered front-end quality checklist with 385 rules across 11 categories, designed for both human review and AI agent workflows.
   Why worth watching: With 72,789 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 18, and a high acceleration of 2.5, the project is experiencing a sudden breakout in attention. The recent release and active commits (within 7 days) suggest ongoing development, while the MCP server integration positions it as a key tool for AI-assisted code review.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward structured, rule-based AI agents that can enforce best practices autonomously. Developers should consider building similar MCP servers for their own standards to enable agent-driven quality assurance.
   Problem solved: Provides a structured, auditable front-end best practices system that developers and AI agents can use to ensure code quality, accessibility, performance, and security, reducing the need to memorize or manually enforce standards.
   What changed: The project recently added an MCP server (mcp.frontendchecklist.io) and skills system, enabling AI agents to use the checklist directly. The README now emphasizes agent workflows, and a companion project (UX Patterns for Devs) was launched. These updates likely drove the sudden spike in stars.
   Target users: Front-end developers, web developers, QA engineers, and AI agent developers who want to automate front-end quality checks.
   Risk notes: The checklist is opinionated and may not cover all edge cases. Over-reliance on automated checks could miss context-specific issues. The MCP server is hosted externally, so availability and latency depend on the provider.
   LLM confidence: high

3. esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   GitHub: https://github.com/esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 18,785 (24h +644, 7d +4799)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1x (high, 前 3 日均值 670.3)
   Score: 83 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
   Why worth watching: With 18,785 stars, a 24-hour delta of +644, and a 7-day average of +735 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of ongoing community interest.
   Developer takeaway: For AI developers, Reasonix signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that prioritize cost efficiency and long-running autonomy. The Go rewrite and plugin architecture suggest a maturing ecosystem where performance and extensibility are key differentiators.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Provides a config-driven, plugin-extensible AI coding assistant that optimizes token costs via DeepSeek's prefix caching, enabling long-running sessions without prohibitive API expenses.
   What changed: The recent 1.0 release (ground-up rewrite in Go) and active development on the main-v2 branch, with frequent commits and a release within 30 days, likely drove renewed attention. The README highlights a migration from legacy TypeScript, which may have attracted users seeking a more performant binary.
   Target users: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent with configurable tools, permissions, and plugin support; also those interested in MCP-compatible agent frameworks.
   Risk notes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching policies, the project's value proposition may weaken. The 214 open issues indicate active but potentially buggy development.
   LLM confidence: high

4. rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   GitHub: https://github.com/rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 29,290 (24h +804, 7d +8190)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.2x (medium, 前 3 日均值 3693)
   Score: 82 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to autonomous swarms, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
   Why worth watching: With 29,096 stars, a 7-day delta of 7,996 stars, and a 3-day average delta of 3,693, this repo is experiencing explosive organic growth. The 24-hour delta of 610 stars and medium acceleration (0.2) indicate sustained, high-velocity attention. The curriculum's promise to bridge the 84% usage vs. 18% preparedness gap resonates strongly as AI adoption accelerates.
   Developer takeaway: The demand for structured, from-scratch AI education is massive and growing. Developers are moving beyond API wrappers and seeking foundational understanding. This repo signals a shift toward 'deep learning' in the literal sense—building internal models of how AI works, not just using black-box tools.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI learning resources are fragmented—papers, blog posts, and demos that don't connect. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end spine that builds understanding from raw math to production agents, closing the gap between AI tool usage and professional preparedness.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-06), and the repo has active commits within 7 days, but the star growth pattern suggests steady viral spread through developer communities and social media.
   Target users: Developers and engineers who want to understand AI deeply, from math to production. Suitable for beginners with coding experience (Python recommended) and intermediate practitioners looking to fill knowledge gaps. Also useful for educators building AI curricula.
   Risk notes: The curriculum is still in progress (503 lessons planned, but not all may be complete). The massive scope could lead to maintenance challenges or uneven quality across phases. Learners should verify lesson completion before relying on it as a sole resource.
   LLM confidence: high

5. CopilotKit/CopilotKit
   GitHub: https://github.com/CopilotKit/CopilotKit
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 33,047 (24h +799, 7d +1203)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 6.7x (high, 前 3 日均值 120)
   Score: 79 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: CopilotKit is a multi-platform SDK for building agent-native applications with Generative UI, shared state, and human-in-the-loop workflows across React, Angular, Vue, React Native, Slack, and Teams.
   Why worth watching: The repo gained 799 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,203 in the last 7 days, with a high acceleration of 6.7 (threshold > 2.0). The three-day average star delta of 120 is double the seven-day average of 59.7, indicating a sudden surge in attention. This breakout suggests a recent catalyst—likely a new release, major integration announcement, or viral social media post—that has propelled the project into a rapid growth phase.
   Developer takeaway: The rapid adoption of AG-UI by major AI companies signals a shift toward standardized agent-user interaction protocols. CopilotKit's multi-platform approach reduces fragmentation, making it a strong candidate for teams building production agentic applications.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Developers need a unified frontend stack to integrate AI agents into any application surface, handling dynamic UI rendering, state synchronization, and user interaction loops without building custom infrastructure.
   What changed: The README mentions recent additions like AG-UI Protocol adoption by Google, LangChain, AWS, Microsoft, Mastra, and PydanticAI; support for Angular, Vue, React Native, Slack, and Microsoft Teams; and early access to Self-Learning agents. A recent release within 30 days and commits within 7 days confirm active development. The combination of enterprise partnerships and expanded platform support likely triggered the breakout.
   Target users: Frontend and full-stack developers building AI-powered applications with React, Angular, Vue, or React Native; teams deploying agents to Slack or Microsoft Teams; enterprises needing human-in-the-loop and self-learning agent capabilities.
   Risk notes: The project is backed by a commercial entity (CopilotKit Cloud/Enterprise), which may introduce licensing or pricing changes. Early access features (Self-Learning, Slack/Teams) are not yet generally available. High star velocity may attract maintenance challenges.
   LLM confidence: high

6. santifer/career-ops
   GitHub: https://github.com/santifer/career-ops
   Category: AI Workflow Automation
   Stars: 49,105 (24h +365, 7d +1258)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 2.2x (high, 前 3 日均值 167.3)
   Score: 78 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An open-source AI-powered job search command center that uses Claude Code and other AI CLIs to evaluate, tailor, and track applications at scale.
   Why worth watching: With 48,963 stars, a 24-hour delta of +223, and a 7-day delta of +1,116, this repo is experiencing sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.3 indicates steady, organic momentum rather than a sudden spike. The project has a recent release and active commits within the last 7 days, signaling ongoing maintenance and community engagement. It's trending because it directly addresses a universal pain point — job hunting — with a practical, AI-driven solution that resonates with a massive audience.
   Developer takeaway: Career-Ops signals a shift toward AI-as-infrastructure for personal productivity. Developers should watch for similar 'AI command center' patterns emerging in other domains (e.g., personal finance, health, learning). The project's architecture — using AI to orchestrate sub-agents, parse unstructured data, and generate structured outputs — is a template for building reliable, user-facing AI tools.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Job seekers waste months manually sifting through hundreds of listings, customizing resumes, and tracking applications. Career-Ops automates the entire pipeline: it scans job portals, evaluates offers against your profile using structured scoring, generates ATS-optimized PDFs, and maintains a single source of truth — all while keeping the human in the loop for final decisions.
   What changed: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The consistent star velocity over the past week (three-day avg +167, seven-day avg +148) and the recent release suggest steady word-of-mouth and community adoption rather than a specific viral event.
   Target users: Job seekers in tech (especially AI/ML roles), career changers, and developers who want to automate their job search without sacrificing quality. Also relevant for recruiters and HR professionals looking to understand AI-driven candidate matching.
   Risk notes: The system relies on third-party AI APIs (Claude, Gemini) which may change pricing or availability. The 'spray-and-pray' warning is important — users must review AI-generated applications to avoid reputational damage. The project's rapid growth may attract low-quality forks or misuse.
   LLM confidence: high

7. nexu-io/open-design
   GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
   Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent
   Stars: 60,047 (24h +1078, 7d +4507)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.8x (high, 前 3 日均值 605.7)
   Score: 78 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Open Design is an open-source, local-first desktop app that turns coding agents into design engines, generating prototypes, decks, images, and videos from natural language prompts.
   Why worth watching: With 60K stars, a 24h delta of 1,078 stars, and a 7-day average of 576 stars/day, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.8 indicates strong momentum, not a sudden spike. The recent 0.9.0 release with built-in Model Router and the launch of the Open Design Fellow program are likely fueling continued attention.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift toward agent-native design tools where the CLI becomes the design engine. For AI developers, it's an opportunity to build plugins, design systems, and integrations that extend Open Design's capabilities, or to adopt its MCP-based architecture for other creative domains.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: It replaces closed, cloud-dependent design tools (like Claude Design or Figma) with a local-first, agent-native workflow where any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) can produce production-ready artifacts (HTML, PDF, PPTX, MP4) using a shared design system and 260+ plugins.
   What changed: The release of version 0.9.0 (with an integrated Model Router that eliminates setup friction) and the announcement of the Open Design Fellow program are the most recent visible triggers. The README also highlights expanded agent support (21 CLIs) and a growing plugin ecosystem.
   Target users: Developers, designers, and product builders who want to generate UI prototypes, presentations, images, and videos using AI agents, especially those already using Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or similar tools.
   Risk notes: The project is still in early stages (0.9.0), so stability and API changes are likely. It relies on external AI agents and API keys, which may introduce cost or availability issues. The large number of open issues (325) suggests active development but also potential rough edges.
   LLM confidence: high

8. PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR
   GitHub: https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR
   Category: RAG / Knowledge Base
   Stars: 80,842 (24h +810, 7d +1861)
   Trend: 突然爆火 | Acceleration: 3.1x (high, 前 3 日均值 263.3)
   Score: 76 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: PaddleOCR is a leading open-source OCR toolkit that converts PDFs and images into structured, LLM-ready data (JSON/Markdown) with state-of-the-art accuracy, supporting 100+ languages.
   Why worth watching: With 80k+ stars, a 24h star delta of 566, and a high acceleration ratio of 4.4, PaddleOCR is experiencing a sudden breakout in attention. The 7-day average star delta of 142.7 and recent release (v3.6.0 on 2026-05-28) indicate strong momentum driven by new capabilities like the PaddleOCR-VL-1.6 model achieving 96.3% accuracy on OmniDocBench.
   Developer takeaway: This signals a shift towards specialized, lightweight VLMs for document parsing over general-purpose models, enabling cost-effective, high-accuracy data extraction for LLM applications.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: It solves the challenge of extracting structured data from unstructured documents (PDFs, images) for AI applications, bridging the gap between visual content and LLMs/RAG systems.
   What changed: The release of PaddleOCR 3.6.0 on 2026-05-28 introduced PaddleOCR-VL-1.6, a lightweight VLM with SOTA accuracy (96.3% on OmniDocBench), enhanced ancient document and rare character recognition, and seamless migration from previous versions. This likely triggered the sudden breakout.
   Target users: AI developers, data engineers, and product builders working on document processing, RAG systems, knowledge base creation, and LLM data pipelines.
   Risk notes: Dependency on PaddlePaddle framework may limit adoption in PyTorch-centric ecosystems; however, recent integration with Hugging Face Transformers mitigates this. Also, the project's rapid release cycle may introduce breaking changes.
   LLM confidence: high

9. affaan-m/ECC
   GitHub: https://github.com/affaan-m/ECC
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 208,931 (24h +1457, 7d +10264)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 0.9x (high, 前 3 日均值 1676.3)
   Score: 75 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with skills, memory, security, and continuous learning.
   Why worth watching: With 208K+ stars, a 24-hour star delta of 2,762, and a 7-day average of 1,647, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.3 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike, suggesting deep community adoption. The recent v2.0.0-rc.1 release (Apr 2026) adds operator workflows, a dashboard GUI, and Rust control-plane alpha, signaling active development and expanding scope.
   Developer takeaway: The rise of ECC signals a shift toward platform-level tooling for AI agents. Developers are moving beyond single-harness configs to cross-harness systems that manage skills, memory, and security centrally. This trend suggests that the next wave of AI developer tools will focus on orchestration and optimization rather than just agent capabilities.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: AI coding agents often lack structured memory, reusable skills, security scanning, and cross-harness portability, leading to repetitive setup, context loss, and inconsistent behavior. ECC provides a unified system to manage and optimize agent workflows across different harnesses.
   What changed: The v2.0.0-rc.1 release introduced operator workflows, a Tkinter dashboard, optimization skill packs, and the ECC 2.0 alpha (Rust control-plane). These features broaden the system from a config pack to a full operator platform, likely driving renewed attention.
   Target users: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, etc.) who want to improve agent performance, maintain context across sessions, enforce security policies, and reuse skills across projects.
   Risk notes: The project is primarily maintained by a single developer (affaan-m), which could pose bus-factor risk despite community contributions. The rapid growth may lead to maintenance challenges or feature bloat. Some features (e.g., ECC Pro, GitHub App) are monetized, which could cause friction for users expecting fully free OSS.
   LLM confidence: high

10. HKUDS/nanobot
   GitHub: https://github.com/HKUDS/nanobot
   Category: AI Agent Framework
   Stars: 43,785 (24h +91, 7d +403)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.4x (high, 前 3 日均值 66)
   Score: 75 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: An ultra-lightweight, open-source personal AI agent that you can truly own, with WebUI, chat channels, tools, memory, MCP, and model routing.
   Why worth watching: With 43,785 stars, a 24h delta of +91, and a 7d delta of +403, the project shows strong, sustained growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.4 indicates steady momentum without a sudden spike. This is a mature, actively maintained project that has been consistently gaining attention for months.
   Developer takeaway: The trend toward lightweight, open-source agent frameworks is accelerating. Developers are moving away from monolithic, closed solutions toward modular, self-hosted agents. nanobot's rapid release cycle and broad provider support signal that the ecosystem is maturing.
   Also on: GitHub
   Problem solved: Most AI agents are either too heavy, closed-source, or tied to a single provider. nanobot gives developers a small, readable core that can be extended with plugins, supports multiple LLM providers, and runs locally or on your own infrastructure.
   What changed: The most recent release v0.2.1 (The Workbench Release) on 2026-06-01 introduced a packaged WebUI with clearer thought/response timelines, live file-edit activity, project workspaces, and broader provider/channel support. The README shows daily updates for the past month, indicating rapid iteration.
   Target users: Python developers building personal or team AI assistants, AI enthusiasts who want to run their own agent, and developers integrating LLMs into chat workflows.
   Risk notes: The project is very new (first release in April 2026) and still has 863 open issues. The rapid pace of changes may lead to instability. The README is promotional and may oversell capabilities. The project is from HKUDS, which may have limited long-term support.
   LLM confidence: high

11. sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   GitHub: https://github.com/sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills
   Category: MCP / Tool Calling
   Stars: 39,891 (24h +143, 7d +749)
   Trend: 持续热门 | Acceleration: 1.3x (high, 前 3 日均值 107.3)
   Score: 75 | Risk: Low
   One-liner: Installable GitHub library of 1,500+ agentic skills for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Antigravity, and more. Includes specialized plugins, installer CLI, bundles, workflows, and official/community skill collections.
   Why worth watching: MCP / Tool Calling 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +143，总 stars 39,891，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。
   Developer takeaway: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。
   Also on: GitHub

   Why worth watching: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +258，总 stars 58,176，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。

3. GitHub 开源 Spec Kit 工具包，用产品规范引导 AI 编码
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/rohanpaul_ai/status/2063246343842501091
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: GitHub 发布开源工具包 Spec Kit，旨在解决 "vibe coding" 的最大弱点--AI 常在规则未明确时就开始编码。它把流程从 "让 AI 直接构建" 改为 "先写产品规范，再让 AI 根据规范实现"。当前 AI 编码模式常因松散提示直接跳入代码，导致需求薄弱、边界遗漏和反复返工。Spec Kit 推动反向流程：先定义产品功能，再澄清差距、制订技术计划、分解任务，最后让 agent 执行。规范成为可执行的开发合约，支持 Copilot、Claude Code、Codex、Gemini、Cursor、Qwen 等 30+ agent 集成。项目已获 109K+ 星标。

5. Persona Atlas：Hugging Face 上的开源人物思维映射工具
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/persona-atlas
   Signal captured
   Category: products
   Summary: Persona Atlas 是一个运行在 Hugging Face Inference Providers 上的开源项目。它通过工具调用代理执行真实网络搜索，生成公众人物的资料、事实清单和风格假设，然后让该人物回答十个关于身份、伦理等开放式问题。每个回答被转化为嵌入向量，从而在向量空间中对不同人物进行距离比较，并基于十个特质锚点绘制热力图。前端采用 Gradio，提供研究、比较和检查代理完整追溯三个标签页，预设多个人物角色，无需 token 即可直接体验。

   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://x.com/NVIDIAAI/status/2063034422698389625

   Summary: 被选为 #CVPR2026 最佳论文决赛作品：来自 NVIDIA Research 的 PixelDiT 在大多数图像生成模型中，预训练的自编码器会在任何扩散发生前压缩图像，导致质量损失在整个流程中累积。 PixelDiT，即像素扩散变换器，完全去掉了这一步骤。它是一个单阶段模型，直接在像素空间中端到端地学习扩散过程。

7. 用Qwen2.5-3B构建多智能体经济体：工程报告
   Source: aihot | Type: curated_trend
   URL: https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim
   Signal captured
   Category: tools
   Summary: 开发者用Qwen2.5-3B构建了五人森林生物多智能体经济体，每个智能体独立运行，通过vLLM部署在Modal，以Gradio为交互窗口。3B模型在100%调用中输出有效JSON，但经济判断能力弱。通过设计稀缺性（食物品种限制、易腐坏、冬季燃料危机）和优化提示词（禁止买入自产物品、给出示例）提升决策质量。15轮模拟中，蜜价从10跌至3、柴价从4涨至7、财富基尼系数从0.14扩至0.38。项目展示了小模型可靠格式化与不可靠推理之间的工程填补。
   Original: Hugging Face：Blog（RSS） - https://huggingface.co/blog/build-small-hackathon/thousand-token-wood-sim
   Why it matters: 开发者用Qwen2.5-3B构建了五人森林生物多智能体经济体，每个智能体独立运行，通过vLLM部署在Modal，以Gradio为交互窗口。3B模型在100%调用中输出有效JSON，但经济判断能力弱。通过设计稀缺性（食物品种限制、易腐坏、冬季燃料危机）和优化提示词（禁止买入自产物品、给出示例）提升决策质量。15轮模拟中，蜜价从10跌至3、柴价从4涨至7、财富基尼系数从0.14扩至0.38。项目展示了小模型可靠格式化与不可靠推理之间的工程填补。

数据说明：
- GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。
- daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。
- Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。
- Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。
